headline: Morgan Stanley’s North Haven BDC sticks to 5% cap as redemption requests reach 10.9%
summary: North Haven Private Income Fund LLC, a non-traded business development company affiliated with Morgan Stanley, honored less than half of investors’ redemption requests in Q1 and stuck to its quarterly limit. The fund will stick to its 5% stated quarterly repurchase cap, according to a March 11 tender offer statement. The total amount of redemptions requested was 10.9% of shares outstanding, or roughly $169 million. In a letter to investors dated March 11, Morgan Stanley Private Credit said that

headline: How The Jet2 (AIM:JET2) Investment Narrative Is Shifting As Analyst Targets Tighten
summary: Jet2’s latest analyst updates centre on price targets drifting from £14.50 toward a tighter £13.25 to £14.00 band, with fair value estimates trimmed from £17.76 to £17.32. JPMorgan and others have kept broadly Neutral or Equal Weight stances while adjusting these targets, which signals fine tuning around expectations rather than a wholesale rethink of the story. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets feed into the evolving Jet2 narrative and what to watch next. Analyst Price...

headline: Morgan Stanley (MS) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
